title: Aleuritia elongata
taxonomy: Aleuritia elongata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Aleuritia, which is a part of the order Gentianales. Within the Rubiaceae family, Aleuritia elongata is further classified as a member of the tribe Psychotrieae. The plant's classification is based on its characteristics, such as its morphology and phylogenetic relationships. By understanding its classification, researchers can better understand the evolutionary history of Aleuritia elongata and its place within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Aleuritia elongata has been found to have potential medicinal uses, although more research is required to confirm its efficacy. benefits: Aleuritia elongata, also known as the golden silk orchid, has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits.
